title: elatostema longipedunculatum
intro: Elatostema longipedunculatum is a type of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, native to Southeast Asia, and commonly known as a type of stinging nettle. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high levels of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Elatostema longipedunculatum has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and has also been reported to have anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to provide its health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
